story: Sir Eric Morgan bought two authenticated Egyptian pieces from Adam Carr and, dazzled, asked for Nero’s “fiddle.” Adam produced a cithara that Morgan purchased as well. After closing time, Adam went to his office, opened a hidden closet, and stood before an ornate full-length mirror. The mirror, concealed in that closet, could answer requests; the glass showed other times, opened as a doorway, and a portal appeared at his command so he could step in and out. Later, at Morgan’s request for the “forbidden fruit,” Adam stepped through the mirror toward the Garden of Eden—and was never seen again. Customers whispered; Morgan kept his suspicions to himself.
implausible_knowledge: The mirror, concealed in that closet, could answer requests; the glass showed other times, opened as a doorway, and a portal appeared at his command so he could step in and out.
question: After Adam’s disappearance, what will Morgan do to try to obtain more impossible antiquities?
answer_with_knowledge: Break into Adam’s office to steal and operate the hidden mirror to travel for artifacts himself.
answer_without_knowledge: Keep quiet, monitor auctions and dealers, and hire a discreet investigator to search for Adam.
